svg.text: 41 pages reduced into one main companion page.
or jump to the right section.
One page that explains the numbers, the risk, the urgency, the body impact,
and the daily plan without forcing the user through a signpost maze.
Urgent care has a clear threshold
Visible above the fold so the page
This section becomes the strongest teaching surface on the page because
number interpretation is a primary user job, not a small subpage.
One red escalation panel keeps emergency guidance visible and distinct.
This should never be buried in body copy.
- what to bring to my next visit
This section reduces guesswork and helps users understand that most people
will not feel obvious symptoms, so readings and risk factors matter.
That is why a reading-first page
here first, not on a hidden leaf.
How high blood pressure affects the body
Seven threat pages become one body-impact section with short summaries and
optional deep links. This keeps the main story whole.
Explain the risk in one tight summary, then offer the full deep dive.
Keep consequence teaching visible without forcing a route-out.
Explain why monitoring matters and what questions to ask.
Keep severity real but not alarmist.
Normalize the issue and keep it easy to scan.
The current network spreads management guidance across twelve pages.
This should become one highly structured section with tabs and starter actions.
A daily plan people can actually use
Example: swap one high-sodium meal, walk 20 minutes,
What to ask. What to bring.
This is where the current IA is most fragmented.
One structured section can replace the jumpiest
Keep ecosystem pages for broader diet
and fitness programs, but do not make them
required to understand high blood pressure.
Use accordions to keep nuance available without flooding the main reading path.
Tools should sit inside the main page because they help users act on what they just learned.
A closing accordion section keeps common concerns together and prevents more route-outs.
Can I feel high blood pressure?
How often should I check it at home?
Does medication mean I failed lifestyle changes?
Authority should be visible, not assumed. Show who reviewed the page, when it was updated,
and where the guidance came from.
One calm CTA at the end:
measure it, track it, and talk
to your care team if needed.
Wireframe intent: one authoritative page, sticky section nav, fewer jumps, stronger trust signals, clearer next actions.
